channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian Budget Focus Needed on Supply-Side Economics Amidst Geopolitical Uncertainty
item.description: With significant financial and geopolitical volatility impacting the global economy, particularly following Donald Trump's actions in Iran, Australian policymakers and businesses are facing heightened...

item.title: Examining the intelligence lapses that allowed the Rwandan genocide to unfold
item.description: The article from Nation Africa focuses on how lapses in intelligence sharing and political inaction contributed to the tragic Rwandan genocide of 1994. It explains that local and international intelli...

item.title: Geordin Hill-Lewis elected as new DA federal leader
item.description: Geordin Hill-Lewis, the 39-year-old mayor of Cape Town, has been elected as the new federal leader of the Democratic Alliance (DA), South Africa’s second-largest political party. His election, announc...

item.title: King Charles urged to deliver a frank message to the United States during his upcoming Congress speech
item.description: Simon Tisdall argues that King Charles III’s forthcoming visit to the United States should be more than mere ceremony. Scheduled to address a joint session of Congress later this month, the king’s app...

item.title: Life and Legacy of Australia's Oldest Postmistress
item.description: Patricia Bennett, Australia's oldest postmistress, retired at 95 after a career spanning nearly four decades in Bellata, a small town in northern central New South Wales. The journey began in 1988 whe...

item.title: New Measurements Confirm Faster-Than-Expected Expansion of the Universe
item.description: A global team of astronomers has achieved the most precise measurement yet of the local Universe's expansion rate, revealing that it is expanding faster than predicted by early-Universe models. This d...

item.title: Magnolia Hotshots secure solid win over undermanned San Miguel in Commissioner's Cup
item.description: Magnolia Hotshots proved too strong for the short-handed San Miguel Beermen in their latest matchup in the PBA Season 50 Commissioner’s Cup held on April 12, 2026. San Miguel played without import pla...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Urges Peace and Protection for Lebanese Civilians Amid Ongoing Middle East War
item.description: Pope Leo XIV don express him deep concern and love for the people of Lebanon this Sunday as the war for Middle East still dey scatter many lives. The 70-year-old pontiff talk say e dey feel very close...

item.title: Geordin Hill-Lewis Becomes New DA Leader
item.description: Cape Town's Mayor Geordin Hill-Lewis has officially taken over as the leader of the Democratic Alliance (DA), succeeding John Steenhuisen, who will continue his role as Minister of Agriculture. The de...

item.title: FEU Lady Tamaraws defeat UP Fighting Maroons to stay in contention for UAAP Final Four
item.description: The Far Eastern University (FEU) Lady Tamaraws regained momentum in the UAAP Season 88 women's volleyball tournament after overcoming the University of the Philippines (UP) Fighting Maroons. The match...

item.title: Eamonn Holmes recovering after suffering a stroke, according to his son
item.description: Television presenter Eamonn Holmes is reportedly on the road to recovery after suffering a stroke, according to comments made by his son. The veteran broadcaster, known for his long career on British ...

item.title: Albanese meets Brunei and Malaysian leaders to secure Australia’s energy and fertiliser imports
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese will visit Brunei and Malaysia this week to secure vital supplies of fuel and fertiliser for Australia amid a global oil crisis. In Brunei, he is set to meet Sultan Haj...

item.title: Nitish Kumar Set to Resign, New Bihar Chief Minister Expected Soon
item.description: So, listen, things are getting a bit interesting in Bihar, no? According to sources, Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar is expected to resign sometime after a crucial cabinet meeting scheduled for Apri...

item.title: Khana LGA Organizes Summit to Empower Ogoni Creatives in May 2026
item.description: The Khana Local Government Area don set to host a two-day seminar called 'KHANA Creative Industry Summit 2026' from May 5th to 6th. The Executive Chairman, Hon. Thomas Bariere, go lead the event. Acco...

item.title: People Who Spend Most of Their Day on Phones and Screens
item.description: The article explores a growing group of individuals referred to as 'screenmaxxers'—people who spend nearly all of their waking hours using digital devices. Profiles include Morgan Dreiss, a copy edito...

item.title: Artemis II Offers a Cosmic Perspective on Earth's Beauty and Fragility
item.description: The Artemis II mission, which sent astronauts farther from Earth than any humans before, has prompted reflections on our planet's beauty and fragility. Echoing the impact of the famous 1968 'Earthrise...

item.title: Four Suspects Arrested Over Murder of British National in Kenya
item.description: Kwa habari kutoka Kwale, maafisa wa DCI wamemkamata watu wanne wanaoshukiwa kuhusika na mauaji ya Campbell Scott, raia wa Uingereza, pamoja na matukio kadhaa ya wizi kwa kutumia nguvu. Operesheni hiyo...

item.title: Ceasefire in Iran War Could Benefit Canadian Energy Exports
item.description: The tentative ceasefire in the recent Iran war presents both risks and opportunities for the global energy market, with Canada positioned to benefit. While the Strait of Hormuz remains a point of unce...

item.title: Richards Bay Focused on Own Goals Amidst Premiership Title Race
item.description: Richards Bay FC have signalled that they won’t be a pawn in the Betway Premiership title race between Orlando Pirates and Mamelodi Sundowns. After a recent draw with the Buccaneers, Pirates find thems...

item.title: Rivers Police Name ASP Agabe Blessing Kaborlo as New Public Relations Officer
item.description: The Rivers State Police Command don announce say Assistant Superintendent of Police (ASP) Agabe Blessing Kaborlo don become the new Police Public Relations Officer (PPRO) for the state. The Inspector-...
